content.claim: OECD's PISA 2022 Volume I identifies Hungary (alongside Romania, Slovakia, Israel and Chinese Taipei) as having the widest performance gaps linked to socio-economic status, with disadvantaged students far more likely not to reach basic proficiency.
content.context: Because early selection is codified as a narrow, separately-justified legal exception rather than the structural default of secondary education, reform of the selection age is legally more tractable than reshaping Hungary's broader maintainer or curriculum architecture — but legitimacy and constitutional doctrine require any change to proceed on a felmenő-rendszer (grade-by-grade phase-in) basis with minimum ~1-year statutory lead time, protecting students already admitted under existing rules.
limitations: The v1 source string was not re-verified during migration.
content.population: Population described in the migrated claim
